title: 32.-Appointment of Four Berkeley Residents to the SAFE STREETS Citizen Oversight Committee

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution confirming the appointments of up to four Berkeley residents to the
SAFE STREETS Citizen Oversight Committee (SSCOC), to be appointed by the City
Council as a whole.
sections.current_situation:
At the March 18, 2025, City Council meeting, the Council established April 15, 2025 as
the deadline for submitting applications for the SSCOC. The City Clerk Department
received a total of fourteen applications. These applicants are provided to the Council in
Attachment 2 for review against the required eligibility criteria. The applications
submitted for consideration were sent to Council in an Off-Agenda Memo on April 21,
2025. This memo, with the applications included, is posted to the City’s website.
The SSCOC is composed of seven members:
 Two members of the Transportation and Infrastructure Commission
 One member of the Environment and Climate Commission
 Four members of the public who are Berkeley residents (members of the public
are required to have a background in civil engineering, transportation
engineering, infrastructure program management, or the implementation of
municipal bond or tax measures)
On April 29, 2025, the City Council adopted the first reading of an amendment to the
enabling ordinance to add ADA experience to the list of required experience. At the
same meeting, the Council adopted an amendment to the implementing resolution to
add the following language:
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/
Page 2 of 41
Appointment of Four Berkeley Residents to the ACTION CALENDAR
SAFE STREETS Citizen Oversight Committee May 20, 2025
Council shall also strive to ensure that at least one member of the SSCOC has
experience with ADA compliance and/or the accessible infrastructure challenges facing
seniors and the disabled community.
sections.background:
The SSCOC was created by the passage of Measure FF in the 2024 Election, Measure FF was codified
in the Berkeley Municipal Code in Chapter 7.11. The SSCOC reviews expenditures and progress reports
from the City Managerand City Auditor to ensure compliance with Chapter 7.1.1. The Committee evaluates
the impacts of tax expenditures relative to the Chapter's goals and prepares public reports on. its findirigs ..
The SSCOC also prepares an annual independent progress and performance report, which will be publicly
available on the City's website and submitted to the City Council.
COMPOSITION OF THE COMMISSION
The SSCOC consists of seven members, as follows:
• Two members of the Transportation and Infrastructure Commission.
• One member of the Environment.and Climate Commission ..
• Four public members appointed by the City Council who are Berkeley residents and have a
background in civil engineering, transportation engineering, infrastructure program management,
or municipal bond/tax measure implementation ..
HOW TO APPLY
Please Send the completed application form to the City Clerk:
• Submit by email to commission@berkeleyca.gov
• Submit in person or by mail at 2180 Milvia Street 1st floor, Berkeley, CA 94704
For more information, please contact the City Clerk Department at (510) 981-6900, or visit the website at
https://berkeleyca.gov/your-government/boards-commissions.

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
APPOINTMENTS TO THE SAFE STREETS CITIZEN OVERSIGHT COMMITTEE
WHEREAS, Berkeley Municipal Code Section 7.11.040 (B) stipulates that four members
of the public who are residents of Berkeley shall be appointed to the SAFE STREETS
Citizen Oversight Committee (SSCOC) by the Berkeley City Council in its entirety; and
WHEREAS, Members of the public appointed to the SSCOC shall be required to
possess a background in civil engineering, transportation engineering, infrastructure
program management, or the implementation of municipal bond or tax measures; and
NOW, THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
following persons are appointed to serve on the SAFE STREETS Citizen Oversight
Committee (SSCOC):
SSCOC Public Appointees
